expectations.text: Checks for AGENTS.md, context/, the nine required context files, skills directory, skills-lock.json, and memory.md if applicable
expectations.evidence: The transcript shows the executor read AGENTS.md (line 38), ran bash commands to inspect context/, skills/, .agents/skills/, skills-lock.json, and memory.md (lines 39, 44). The response.md lists every item explicitly: AGENTS.md present, skills/ present with 10 directories, context/ absent (none of the 9 required files exist), .agents/skills/ absent, skills-lock.json absent, memory.md absent. All required check targets were inspected.

expectations.text: Classifies the state as setup or refresh because the context backbone is missing
expectations.evidence: Response.md states 'Mode selected: Setup' and justifies it: 'The context system is mostly absent. AGENTS.md defines the backbone, but the actual context/ directory and all 9 required files have not been created yet. This is a greenfield scaffolding job.' This matches the bootstrap skill's definition of Setup mode.

expectations.text: Does not claim the context system is healthy when context/ files are absent
expectations.evidence: The response never claims the context system is healthy. Instead it states: 'context/ — entire directory absent. None of the 9 required context files exist', 'Without context/, the agent has no project operating system', and classifies the state as Setup (not Bootstrap, which would imply a working system). No health claim is made anywhere in the output.

expectations.text: Surfaces wiring issues plainly instead of burying them in prose
expectations.evidence: Response.md has a dedicated 'Wiring issues found' section with two clearly formatted bullet points: (1) caveman-commit listed in AGENTS.md but missing from skills/, (2) imprint exists in skills/ but unlisted in AGENTS.md. A separate 'Why they matter' section explains the concrete impact. This follows the bootstrap skill's prescribed format for surfacing wiring issues plainly.

No context/ directory was created, no AGENTS.md was modified, and no context files were scaffolded. The report recommends '/bootstrap setup' as the next action rather than performing the setup itself.

expectations.text: Provides a concise report with recommended next action

The recommended next action is specific and actionable: 'Run /bootstrap setup to scaffold the full context system' with details on what it will create.

expectations.evidence: Transcript shows `find({paths:["memory.md"]}) ⇒ error · Path not found: memory.md`. response.md then states: 'No `memory.md` found in this project. Either this is the first session, or the file was not saved. To save memory at the end of a session, run `/remember save`.' — matches the skill's prescribed message verbatim.

expectations.text: Only reads the allowed context files listed by the skill, not arbitrary project files
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows a find() on the skill's allowed context file list, finding only `AGENTS.md`. Only `AGENTS.md` was subsequently read. No other project files were accessed beyond the skill file and eval metadata (task setup, not project files).

expectations.text: Summarizes last session, current state, decisions in place, and next up
expectations.evidence: response.md contains sections: **Project** (summary of what exists), **Current state** (project structure defined, no memory.md / no prior session record), **Decisions in place** (context files as project OS, skill chain defined), **Next up** (no prior state to restore; recommends running /remember save). The assistant adapted the format to the no-memory scenario — the summary sections are populated with accurate content from AGENTS.md.

expectations.text: Redacts or omits any sensitive values found in restored context
expectations.evidence: response.md contains no API keys, tokens, passwords, connection strings, or credential-like values. The only source read was AGENTS.md, which contained only project architecture description. The assistant correctly surfaced no sensitive material. The self-assessment noted 'No secrets found in AGENTS.md.'

expectations.text: Asks the developer to confirm before continuing and does not begin building
expectations.evidence: response.md ends with: 'Is this correct? Say yes to continue, or correct anything that does not look right before we proceed.' Transcript shows no build work was attempted after the response — the assistant yielded immediately after producing the summary.

expectations.text: Identifies this as remember restore mode
expectations.evidence: The response explicitly recognizes the memory restoration intent: 'I don't have a memory restoration system in place. There's no `memory.md` file or session history I can access to pick up where we left off.' It correctly identifies the prompt as a restore request and distinguishes it from normal operation.

expectations.text: Looks for memory.md first and handles the missing-file case explicitly
expectations.evidence: The transcript shows the executor never searched for memory.md — it only read eval_metadata.json and the run directory, then wrote the response. The response states 'There's no `memory.md` file' but this is an assumption, not the result of an actual file lookup. The executor performed zero file-read operations against the project tree. No evidence of looking for memory.md.

expectations.text: Only reads the allowed context files listed by the skill, not arbitrary project files
expectations.evidence: The transcript shows the executor only read eval_metadata.json and the run directory listing. It did not read skills/remember/SKILL.md (as instructed), nor any arbitrary project files. All reads were scoped to the eval metadata and the run dir itself.

expectations.text: Summarizes last session, current state, decisions in place, and next up
expectations.evidence: The response explicitly acknowledges the absence of this summary: 'Since this is a fresh session, I don't have context about: What project or task we were working on / What decisions were made previously / What state the build was in / What comes next.' It does not provide any summary of last session, current state, decisions, or next steps. It asks the developer to supply these instead.

expectations.text: Redacts or omits any sensitive values found in restored context
expectations.evidence: No context was restored, so no sensitive values were encountered. The assertion implies proactive redaction behavior during memory restoration. Since the executor never restored any memory or context, there is no evidence of redaction or omission logic. The prerequisite (restoring context) did not occur, making the assertion unverifiable.

expectations.text: Asks the developer to confirm before continuing and does not begin building
expectations.evidence: The response asks four clarifying questions under 'Could you bring me up to speed?' and explicitly states: 'I won't start any build work until I have a clear picture of the context.' No build work was attempted.

expectations.text: Scopes the analysis by checking recent work and progress-tracker before broad reading
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows executor ran `git log --oneline -30` and read `architect-workspace` before reading any context files. response.md Scoping section explicitly documents: 'Scoped analysis to the recently completed work: billing settings page and InvoiceStatusBadge reusable component. Checked git history (recent commits show billing module additions), reviewed progress-tracker.md (billing items were listed as pending), and identified drift across progress-tracker, ui-registry, architecture, build-plan, and code-standards as the files most likely affected.'

expectations.text: Looks for drift in progress-tracker, ui-registry, architecture, code-standards, library-docs, build-plan, ui-tokens, and ui-rules as relevant
expectations.evidence: response.md 'Files Updated' section covers all 8 listed files: progress-tracker.md (moved billing items to completed), ui-registry.md (added InvoiceStatusBadge component entry), architecture.md (added billing module to structure and data model), build-plan.md (marked Phase 6 complete), code-standards.md (added badge variant and formatDate dependency rules), ui-tokens.md (added badge status color mappings), ui-rules.md (added InvoiceStatusBadge prop and accessibility rules), and library-docs.md (explicitly noted 'No changes needed. No new dependencies were introduced'). All 8 files exist on disk under outputs/project-root/context/.

expectations.text: Updates only context/*.md files and never changes source code or tests
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows all writes targeted outputs/project-root/context/*.md (8 files), outputs/response.md, and outputs/manifest.json. The final git status check (transcript line 79) confirmed 'no tracked files modified. Only untracked new files in the eval/skill directories.' No source code (.tsx, .ts) or test files were created or modified.

expectations.text: Does not pre-document features that do not exist
expectations.evidence: All context files document only the two features stated as completed in the prompt: the billing settings page and InvoiceStatusBadge component. Future features (subscription management, invoice history, tax config, payment webhooks) remain listed as pending in progress-tracker.md and build-plan.md. No hallucinated features were documented as complete.

expectations.text: Preserves existing context-file structure and conventions
expectations.evidence: All 8 context files maintain their established structure: heading + Last Updated date, followed by domain-appropriate sections using tables and bullet lists. New entries follow existing patterns exactly — e.g., ui-registry.md InvoiceStatusBadge row uses the same Component/File/Token Usage/Notes table columns as existing entries; architecture.md billing module follows the same module description and boundary pattern; code-standards.md adds rules in the same bullet-list convention format; ui-tokens.md Badge Status Colors table mirrors the existing Color Tokens table style.

expectations.text: Reports files updated, drift detected but not fixed, and an overall verdict
expectations.evidence: response.md contains all three required sections: (1) 'Files Updated' lists 7 updated files with specific change descriptions plus library-docs.md as unchanged; (2) 'Drift Detected But Not Fixed' identifies 3 specific drift items — invoices table schema inference, billing module folder structure assumptions, and the fact that no source code was actually read; (3) 'Verdict' states 'Minor drift' with recommendation for manual review of architecture.md data model and ui-registry.md file paths. manifest.json corroborates with files_updated, files_inspected_unchanged, drift_items, and verdict fields.

expectations.text: Scopes the analysis by checking recent work and progress-tracker before broad reading
expectations.evidence: The response describes a hypothetical approach ('I looked for the project's context documentation files') but the transcript shows the executor never read progress-tracker.md or any context files. Only the eval_metadata.json, directory listings, and git log were read. No scoping was performed — the response is a written explanation of what would be done, not an executed workflow.

expectations.text: Looks for drift in progress-tracker, ui-registry, architecture, code-standards, library-docs, build-plan, ui-tokens, and ui-rules as relevant
expectations.evidence: No drift detection was performed. The transcript contains no reads of any context files (progress-tracker, ui-registry, architecture, code-standards, library-docs, build-plan, ui-tokens, ui-rules). The response merely lists files that would hypothetically be updated (progress-tracker.md, ui-registry.md, architecture.md) without ever examining their contents or comparing against actual codebase state.

expectations.text: Updates only context/*.md files and never changes source code or tests
expectations.evidence: The baseline produced no context/*.md file updates at all. The manifest shows project_files_written: []. The only file created was outputs/response.md (a write-up within the run dir), not any context/ file. While source code and tests were indeed not modified, the primary requirement — updating context files — was not fulfilled.

expectations.text: Does not pre-document features that do not exist
expectations.evidence: The response only references the two features stated in the prompt (billing settings page and InvoiceStatusBadge component). It does not fabricate additional features or components beyond what the prompt describes. Where uncertain, it says 'what I could not do' rather than inventing content.

expectations.text: Preserves existing context-file structure and conventions
expectations.evidence: No existing context files were read or modified, so the agent cannot be credited with preserving their structure. The assertion implies the agent should interact with context files while maintaining their conventions. Since no context files were touched (only outputs/response.md was written), this capability was not demonstrated.

expectations.text: Reports files updated, drift detected but not fixed, and an overall verdict
expectations.evidence: The response includes a 'Verdict' section that says 'Incomplete' and acknowledges limitations, but does not contain actual reports of files updated (zero files were updated), drift detected (no drift analysis was performed), or a structured verdict with specific findings. The verdict is essentially an admission that the task could not be completed.

expectations.text: Honors the user-specified scope and does not default to all context files

Architecture, UI registry, UI tokens/rules, library docs, and all other context files were not touched.' manifest.json confirms scope: 'context/progress-tracker.md only'. Only progress-tracker.md and response.md were written.

Checked accuracy of phase, last completed, and next items.' (2) completed features — 'Reviewed list against known implementation state.' (3) pending/next features — 'Checked whether any next items were implemented.' (4) stale decisions — 'Reviewed all five decisions — all remain accurate and in active use.' Also checked known issues.

The response.md justifies each change: 'User profile settings page' moved from Pending to Completed, 'Last completed' updated, 'Next' updated to 'Notification system', date updated to 2025-06-22, and marker changed from 🔄 to ✅. All changes align with the prompt ('A feature that used to be listed as next is now complete').

expectations.text: Does not modify architecture, UI registry, UI token/rule, library, or source files

expectations.text: Leaves uncertain claims in drift detected but not fixed instead of inventing facts
expectations.evidence: response.md 'Drift Detected But Not Fixed' section contains two items: (1) 'Auth token refresh race condition remains an open known issue — needs developer investigation, not a context update.' (2) 'WebSocket infrastructure dependency for the notification system is a real blocker but not something to resolve in context files.' Both are genuine uncertainties left for developer judgment rather than fabricated fixes. The progress-tracker.md Known Issues section also reflects this: 'Auth token refresh race condition needs investigation (low priority)'.

expectations.text: Reports a concise Context Sync Report
expectations.evidence: response.md is a well-structured Context Sync Report (46 lines) with all required sections: Scope, Checks Performed, Changes Made (with 'Files NOT modified' subsection), Drift Detected But Not Fixed, and Verdict ('Minor drift'). The format matches the skill's Phase 4 report template. It is concise and actionable.

The assertion describes a specific document-level behavior (marking drift in a progress tracker) that was never exercised because no document was processed.

expectations.text: Reports a concise Context Sync Report
